title: Polish remaining terminology and harden remaining gates
outOfScope: Changing application runtime behavior outside the gates under review
Expanding beyond terminology, supply-chain evidence, and coverage thresholds

sources.reason: Active work item record defines the task boundary.
Primary entrypoint for the template and installation wording.
Contains the adoption and calibration instructions being refined.
Defines the coverage gate and supply-chain check entry points.
Implements the supply-chain evidence and vulnerability audit checks.

riskAssessment.reason: The task spans documentation wording plus a small set of control-plane gates.

executionDecision.reason: The task is a bounded documentation and gate-hardening pass with clear scope and acceptance.

preReviewWarnings: Keep template identity language intact while removing draft-like wording and harden only the remaining gates.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The remaining draft-like template/preset/placeholder wording is reduced to the final small set identified in the scan.
Template identity remains explicit in the repository metadata and documentation.
The supply-chain audit gate covers the lockfile, SBOM/provenance baselines, and vulnerability scanning.
The project coverage floor is raised from the current placeholder baseline to a stricter value that still passes on the current repository.
The documentation tests covering metadata and release behavior continue to pass.
guidelines: Keep the repository positioned as an AI framework template.
Do not change runtime behavior outside the remaining gates under review.
Do not add runtime dependencies; dev-only tooling may be added when required to close the remaining gates.

intent.problem: The documentation still contains a few draft-like references to template, preset, and placeholder wording, and the remaining control-plane gaps are a stricter coverage floor and a dependency vulnerability audit gate.
constraints: Preserve the template positioning of the repository.
Limit the change set to terminology, supply-chain evidence, and coverage gating.
Do not change product runtime behavior.
intent.rationale: A final terminology pass plus the remaining gate hardening will make the docs read like stable operational guidance while closing the last meaningful control-plane gaps.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: docs still communicate template identity after terminology cleanup
evidence: README and installation docs keep the template and calibration model explicit.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: draft-like template/preset/placeholder wording is reduced to the remaining intended uses
evidence: The remaining occurrences are limited to true template identity, calibration guidance, and test fixtures.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: supply-chain gate checks lockfile, SBOM/provenance, and dependency vulnerability results
evidence: check_supply_chain validates the lockfile and generated evidence artifacts.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: coverage gate enforces a stricter but still passing repository floor
evidence: The repository currently exceeds the planned stricter floor, so the gate can be raised without breaking the current suite.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly asked to continue the remaining gate hardening work, including Makefile updates.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
